mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 100k signals weakening labor market conditions, which triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% and housing starts (HOUST) above 700k signals strong demand pressures, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL > 2% and HOUST > 700k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 95 signals weakening manufacturing activity, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening of monetary policy (FEDFUNDS rising above 2%) combined with slowing money supply growth (RSAFS declining below 400k) predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where both FEDFUNDS > 2% and RSAFS < 400k.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 8% predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to reduced consumer spending and corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances when UNRATE > 8%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly acceleration in GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as firms benefit from stronger economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances when GDPC1 > 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 95 combined with rising oil prices (not in catalog) would signal supply shocks, but since oil prices are unavailable, this hypothesis uses INDPRO alone to predict negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances when INDPRO < 95.
